content: The generic Self-Refine framework for unsupervised LLM output improvement can be augmented by integrating multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to break through the local optimization traps that single-perspective self-feedback often falls into. This creates a new hybrid self-improvement paradigm that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of needing no additional training or labeled data while fixing its limitation of stagnating at suboptimal outputs.
